# Transcrição: 10. Not Organized to Store Data

- **Fonte:** 10. Not Organized to Store Data
- **Duração:** 01:24
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:24]

not organized to store data, not committed to storing data, not storing critical contact of customers. Look this is one of probably three of the biggest mistakes that I've made in my career was not storing data of customers since the very beginning. I didn't have a CRM in place, okay, I wasn't collecting the data in my phones, I wasn't committed to keeping all the data no matter what for my entire existence. You should never discard contact data, always update the contacts, contacts or contracts, keep your data, keep it as critical. Look your contacts are money, even if you move on, you go from one job to another job, okay, and you're like I'll never go back and do that again. Do not get rid of the contacts, keep them organized in a CRM, keep them organized where you can get to them, keep that critical data that's important, never, never, never let go of it because it doesn't matter what you were doing a year or two ago, it doesn't matter what you were doing at the last business. You're not in that business anymore but you're still in this business, the business of people, the business of taking care of people, the business of bringing a great attitude, great service, great value, and the people that did business with you three years ago or 30 years ago, hey, those are good contacts with you and you're gonna need them. Never get rid of old contacts, it's a big mistake made in follow-up, don't you make it.
